items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

children.content: The WA Police commissioner writes to Meta to ask for assistance with Facebook moderation after a number of derogatory comments are left on social media pages.

A photo over someone's shoulder of them holding a phone with the WA Police Force facebook page on it.

items.title: AFL heavyweights Fremantle, Sydney primed for showdown

children.content: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium on Thursday night, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

image.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

items.title: Foreign investor snub of divestment order 'extraordinary', says expert

children.content: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

image.altText: A remote mining camp in the outback

items.title: Millions of tiny engineers help to restore health of marine ecosystem in WA

children.content: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

items.title: BHP workers to strike as negotiations with mining giant stall

children.content: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

image.altText: A water tank with the words 'Welcome to Port Hedland' and the BHP logo

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

children.content: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

image.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: The 'amazing' turnaround in literacy at this primary school

children.content: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school just outside Bunbury in south-west WA could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

items.title: Hastie calls for 'third way' beyond multiculturalism and monoculturalism

children.content: Liberal frontbencher Andrew Hastie says the debate about monoculturalism and multiculturalism is "woke" and that Australia needs a "third way". Follow live.

image.altText: Andrew Hastie, wearing a dark suit and a white shirt, speaks in a news studio

items.title: 'Twenty minutes or so': The blunt warning of China's nuclear capability

children.content: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

image.altText: A Chinese man in a suit sitting in a room with wooden panels and a leather sofa in the background.

items.title: Principal warns school could be forced to close after bus assistance cut

children.content: The head of an independent school in regional WA says changes to student travel subsidies could ultimately lead to the institution's demise.

items.title: New South Wales writes a new fate in blood after epic decider

children.content: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

image.altText: A rugby league player in blue holds up his hands in celebration

items.title: ABC rejects claims of antisemitic bias ahead of fronting royal commission

children.content: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

image.altText: A sign shown on the outside of a large building.

items.title: Racist commentary called out in World Cup coverage

children.content: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

items.title: 'Can't believe it': British wildcard books Wimbledon semifinal spot

children.content: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

image.altText: Arthur Fery doffs his cap to the crowd after his win

items.title: Cost-of-living crisis 'hits from two directions' as major musicals close

children.content: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

items.title: Emmy nominations 2026 revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list

children.content: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

items.title: 'Completely unimaginable': Man critically injured in robot vacuum fire

children.content: The family of a Perth man critically injured when a robot vacuum caught fire at his home say their lives have been "turned upside down" by the "horrific" incident.

items.title: New varieties set Australian coffee industry up for expansion

children.content: Australians are among the world's biggest coffee drinkers but less than 1 per cent of the coffee consumed nationwide is grown locally. That could be about to change.

image.altText: Coffee pouring into shot glass from espresso machine

items.title: Discounts 'too little, too late' after slow start to WA's northern tourist season

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals are upon us. Here's what you need to know

items.title: Labor in 'fight for our lives' as by-election opens door to One Nation assault

children.content: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaking at a lecturn

items.title: Widow not told of husband's death on FIFO swing until 14 hours later

children.content: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant more than 18 months ago.
